lede: A look at June 17 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including M. C. Escher and Igor Stravinsky, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: June 2017 Portugal wildfires
events.desc: A series of wildfires in central Portugal kill at least 64 people and injure 204 others.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: RMS Lancastria is attacked and sunk by the Luftwaffe near Saint-Nazaire, France. At least 3,000 are killed in Britain's worst maritime disaster.

events.title: Boxer Rebellion
events.desc: Boxer Rebellion: Western Allied and Japanese forces capture the Taku Forts in Tianjin, China.

events.title: American Civil War
events.desc: American Civil War: Battle of Vienna, Virginia.

events.title: France
events.desc: In France, the Third Estate declares itself the National Assembly.

events.title: American Revolutionary War
events.desc: American Revolutionary War: Colonists inflict heavy casualties on British forces while losing the Battle of Bunker Hill.

events.title: Mumtaz Mahal
events.desc: Mumtaz Mahal dies during childbirth. Her husband, Mughal emperor Shah Jahan I, will spend the next 17 years building her mausoleum, the Taj Mahal.

events.title: Francis Drake
events.desc: Sir Francis Drake claims a land he calls Nova Albion (modern California) for England.

events.title: Kalmar Union
events.desc: The Kalmar Union is formed under the rule of Margaret I of Denmark.

events.title: Pope Martin I
events.desc: Pope Martin I is arrested and taken to Constantinople, due to his opposition to monothelitism.

deaths.text: Mohamed Morsi, Egyptian professor and politician, first elected president of Egypt after Egyptian revolution

observances.text: Christian feast day: Hypatius of Bithynia (Eastern Orthodox and Byzantine Catholic Churches)
Christian feast day: Samuel and Henrietta Barnett (Church of England)
